mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in existing home sales and new housing starts indicate a collapsing housing sector that drags down consumer spending and financial stability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns (IC < 0.01) or positive returns occur in >60% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-shock recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero correlation with 5-day forward returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction without regime filtering.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly translates to stronger corporate revenue fundamentals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates exhibit no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 290, persistent inflation forces monetary tightening expectations that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 290, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive returns (IC < 0.01) or shows equal predictive power when analyzed independently.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during stagflationary environments signals supply constraints and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high-inflation periods fail to predict negative returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or positive returns dominate in >55% of cases.
